titleEn: Nationwide protests disrupt trading at Joburg municipal market
titleZh: 全国性抗议活动扰乱约翰内斯堡市政市场交易
summaryZh: 南非全国性抗议活动针对来自非洲其他国家的移民（无论是否合法），导致约翰内斯堡市政生鲜市场交易中断。极端组织设定最后期限要求“无证”外国人离境，实际演变为针对所有非洲外国人的仇外攻击。市场交易员、农民和运输商因安全担忧而停工，许多买家为外国人，交易量大幅下降。政府正通过多个边境口岸加速驱逐数千人。文章还提及巴西果农探索低流量无人机喷洒技术、沙特解除黎巴嫩农产品禁令等农业新闻。
featuredReason: 尽管本文主要涉及南非社会政治事件，但其中提及的巴西果农探索低流量无人机喷洒技术、温室投资洞察等农业科技内容，以及市场动荡对生鲜供应链的影响，对智慧畜牧行业从业者理解全球农业市场动态和技术趋势具有参考价值。
contentFull: Nationwide protests disrupt trading at Joburg municipal market Nationwide protests against the presence of Africans from the rest of the continent, whether documented or not, are keeping many South Africans home today. Adherents to populist organisations are congregating "to close all spaza shops and clean up" neighbourhoods of "all Foreign Nationals to make space for South Africans". Extremist groups had set today as the deadline for 'undocumented' foreigners to leave the country, but in practice, all African foreigners are open to attack as xenophobia is normalised. After years of a laissez-faire attitude to the well-known existence of thousands of 'illegal immigrants', while setting near-impossible hoops for earnest seekers of work permits and residence permits, the South African government is now scrambling to deport thousands of people either through the Beit Bridge border post (mostly Zimbabweans and Malawians), Komati post into Mozambique, and OR Tambo Airport (Ghanaians, Nigerians). For decades, resentment towards other Africans has sporadically bubbled over into attacks as economic and political refugees from across the continent are blamed for high unemployment and crime, often a convenient scapegoat employed by populist politicians such as former president Zuma's new political party. Concerns about the drug trade play a large role in residents' anger against foreign nationals; a current commission of enquiry has laid open the close relationship between corrupt top police officers and drug gangs. It is no coincidence that increasingly anti-foreigner marches have taken root in KwaZulu-Natal, flashpoint of the 2021 civil unrest. Zulu nationalism (and possible secessionist ambitions) is strongly linked to the new political party created by disgraced former president Zuma, no longer welcome in the ANC. © Carolize Jansen | FreshPlaza.com Johannesburg fresh produce market "By 8 o'clock this morning, most agents at the Joburg fresh produce market had gone home," says a market floor trader. "Most farmers and transporters are concerned for the safety of employees and vehicles, and they've all decided to avoid being on the road today." Many of the buyers at the market are not South African, and account for much of the trading volumes. "Making the country ungovernable" is a common threat, a tried and tested manoeuvre from the anti-apartheid playbook and under the ANC government adopted by citizens whose needs and requirements are ignored by a self-absorbed ruling party. translationZh:
全国性抗议活动扰乱约翰内斯堡市政市场交易
全国性抗议活动针对来自非洲其他国家的移民（无论是否合法），导致今天许多南非人留在家中。民粹主义组织的追随者聚集起来，“关闭所有spaza商店并清理”社区中的“所有外国国民，为南非人腾出空间”。极端组织将今天设定为“无证”外国人离境的最后期限，但实际上，随着仇外心理正常化，所有非洲外国人都面临攻击风险。多年来，南非政府对数千名“非法移民”的存在采取放任态度，同时为认真寻求工作许可和居留许可的人设置几乎不可能逾越的障碍，如今政府正匆忙通过贝特桥边境口岸（主要针对津巴布韦人和马拉维人）、科马蒂口岸进入莫桑比克，以及奥利弗·坦博国际机场（针对加纳人和尼日利亚人）驱逐数千人。几十年来，对其他非洲人的怨恨偶尔会爆发为攻击，因为来自非洲大陆各地的经济和政治难民被指责为高失业率和犯罪的原因，这往往是民粹主义政客（如前总统祖马的新政党）使用的方便替罪羊。对毒品贸易的担忧在居民对外国人的愤怒中扮演重要角色；当前的一个调查委员会揭露了腐败的高级警官与毒品团伙之间的密切关系。反外国人游行在夸祖鲁-纳塔尔省日益扎根并非巧合，该省是2021年内乱的爆发点。祖鲁民族主义（以及可能的分离主义野心）与失宠的前总统祖马创建的新政党密切相关，祖马已不再受非国大欢迎。
© Carolize Jansen | FreshPlaza.com
约翰内斯堡生鲜市场
“到今天早上8点，约翰内斯堡生鲜市场的大多数代理商已经回家了，”一位市场交易员说。“大多数农民和运输商担心员工和车辆的安全，他们都决定今天避免上路。”市场上的许多买家不是南非人，他们占了交易量的大部分。“让国家无法治理”是一种常见的威胁，这是从反种族隔离斗争剧本中借鉴的经过验证的策略，在非国大政府下，被需求和要求被自我陶醉的执政党忽视的公民所采用。

Adherents to populist organisations are congregating "to close all spaza shops and clean up" neighbourhoods of "all Foreign Nationals to make space…
